rulings.Case Title: ABEL GONZALEZ VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 13, 2011, Line 2, DEFENDANT DR. COLFAX, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants satisfied their burden of production and Plaintiff has not presented substantial evidence to establish a triable issue. The undisputed evidence demonstrates: (1) the Department of Public Health's nondiscriminatory reason for Plaintiff's termination was not pretextual and (2) Dr. Colfax's conduct is not actionable. Plaintiff concedes the Department of Public Health cannot be held liable for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. 1977 SERVICES CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 13, 2011, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted as to issues 1, 3, 5, and 9 and moot as to the remaining issues. Defendant cannot establish the causes of action for breach of contract or bad faith or the request for punitive damages. In addition, defendant is not entitled to a declaration that it is owed further policy benefits because the undisputed facts demonstrate the contrary. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that (1) defendant was legally obligated to pay out the undisbursed funds to the contractors; and (2) the undisbursed funds exceeded the amount that plaintiff ultimately paid the contractors. Policy Exclusion 3(a) applies because defendant "created" or "suffered" the lien by failing to pay the undisbursed funds. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. FINANCE AMERICA, LLC C/O MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Set Aside Judge Pro Tem Rebecca Louise Woodson'S March 29, 2012 Discovery Order For Judcial Misconduct And To Amend Complaint To Add Rebecca Louise Woodson As Doe 1; Memo Of P&A In Support; Decl Of Pltf Vasiliki Serveta In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 13, 2011, Line 14, PLAINTIFF VASILIKI SERVETA'S MOTION To Set Aside Judge Pro Tem Rebecca Louise Woodson'S March 29, 2012 Discovery Order For JudICIal Misconduct And To Amend Complaint To Add Rebecca Louise Woodson As Doe 1. Off calendar. The appeal of the order sought to be vacated deprives the court of jurisdiction to grant the relief sought. Moreover, if the court had jurisdiction to grant the relief, the motion would be denied because a relationship with a lender is not grounds for disqualification of a judicial officer. (Rothman, California Judicial Conduct Handbook (2007) p. 323 at n. 112). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL MATHEWS VS. CLIVE ARMOUR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 13, 2011, Line 16, DEFENDANT CLIVE ARMOUR'S AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied in its entirety. A motion to quash is not an appropriate remedy for proper, albeit untimely, service. Sanctions are inappropriate as Defendant has not shown that the delay caused him any prejudice or to incur any additional expense Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KEITH CUNNINGHAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY, A et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 13, 2011, Line 11, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY, MERCURY GENERAL CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied in its entirety. The allegations regarding the Mezzo settlement are not irrelevant or false within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 436. Those allegations support plaintiffs' bad faith claim and are not barred by Evidence Code 1152. Nor are the waiver and estoppel allegations irrelevant or false. rulings.Case Title: ROTHBERG, REBECCA INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF VS. CRUNCH LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 13, 2011, Line 12, DEFENDANTS CRUNCH LLC, NEFC CRUNCH LLC, AGT AQUISITION WIND DOWN LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first, third, fourth, sixth, and seventh causes of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second, fifth, and eighth causes of action. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to demonstrate Plaintiff did not receive uninterrupted and unrestricted meal breaks and/ or rest periods. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to demonstrate Plaintiff did not receive wages upon her termination. As to the eighth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege that the Labor and Workforce Development Agency notified the parties that it did not intend to investigate. The other causes of action are adequately alleged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
